Free-form positioning so they don't look\n     like a grid \u2014 feels like a designed page, not a debug screen. */\n  .stage-grid {\n    max-width: 980px;\n    margin: 0 auto;\n    display: grid;\n    grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;\n    grid-template-rows: 220px 220px 220px;\n    gap: 24px;\n  }\n\n  .stage {\n    position: relative;\n    background: #000;\n    overflow: hidden;\n    border: 1px solid var(--border);\n    cursor: pointer;\n    transition: border-color 0.18s ease;\n  }\n  .stage:hover {\n    border-color: var(--accent);\n  }\n  .stage > iframe {\n    position: absolute;\n    inset: 0;\n    width: 100%;\n    height: 100%;\n    border: 0;\n    /* Hide the inner watercolor's own controls & bars \u2014 we drive the\n       sims from the parent page via the factory API. */\n    pointer-events: none;\n  }\n  /* Caption tag bottom-left of each stage */\n  .stage .caption {\n    position: absolute;\n    bottom: 8px;\n    left: 8px;\n    z-index: 2;\n    background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.55);\n    color: #f0e8d8;\n    padding: 4px 10px;\n    border-radius: 3px;\n    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;\n    font-size: 10.5px;\n    letter-spacing: 0.06em;\n    pointer-events: none;\n  }\n  /* \"Loading\u2026\" overlay shown until the iframe posts wc:ready. */\n  .stage .loading {\n    position: absolute;\n    inset: 0;\n    z-index: 1;\n    display: flex;\n    align-items: center;\n    justify-content: center;\n    color: var(--ink-muted);\n    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;\n    font-size: 11px;\n    letter-spacing: 0.10em;\n    background: #1a1815;\n    transition: opacity 0.3s ease;\n  }\n  .stage.ready .loading { opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; }\n\n  /* === The five shapes ===\n     #1 big square (upper-left, spans 2 rows)\n     #2 rotated rectangle (top center)\n     #3 circle (top right)\n     #4 tall portrait (right, spans 2 rows)\n     #5 wide letterbox (bottom)\n  */\n\n  #stage-1 {\n    grid-column: 1;\n    grid-row: 1 / span 2;\n  }\n  #stage-2 {\n    grid-column: 2;\n    grid-row: 1;\n    transform: rotate(-4deg);\n    transform-origin: center;\n  }\n  #stage-3 {\n    grid-column: 3;\n    grid-row: 1;\n    border-radius: 50%;\n    /* Circle needs aspect ratio preserved when inside grid */\n    aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;\n    /* Center within its grid cell */\n    justify-self: center;\n    align-self: center;\n    width: 220px;\n    height: 220px;\n  }\n  #stage-4 {\n    grid-column: 3;\n    grid-row: 2 / span 2;\n  }\n  #stage-5 {\n    grid-column: 1 / span 2;\n    grid-row: 3;\n  }\n\n  /* The circular stage's iframe needs to be larger than the visible\n     circle so that simulation-boundary artifacts (cells at the edge\n     of the sim grid) stay outside the visible area \u2014 same trick the\n     standalone Watercolor uses with its -2.5vh inset. */\n  #stage-3 > iframe {\n    width: 130%;\n    height: 130%;\n    inset: -15%;\n  }\n\n  /* Bottom note */\n  footer {\n    max-width: 980px;\n    margin: 48px auto 0;\n    color: var(--ink-muted);\n    font-size: 14px;\n    text-align: center;\n  }\n  footer code {\n    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;\n    font-size: 12.5px;\n    color: var(--accent);\n  }\n\n  @media (max-width: 800px) {\n    .stage-grid {\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n      grid-template-rows: repeat(5, 200px);\n    }\n    #stage-1 { grid-column: 1 / span 2; grid-row: 1; }\n    #stage-2 { grid-column: 1; grid-row: 2; }\n    #stage-3 { grid-column: 2; grid-row: 2; width: auto; height: auto; aspect-ratio: 1; }\n    #stage-4 { grid-column: 1; grid-row: 3 / span 2; }\n    #stage-5 { grid-column: 2; grid-row: 3 / span 2; }\n  }\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n\n<header>\n  <h1>Five Watercolor instances, one page</h1>\n  <p>\n    Each stage below hosts its own Watercolor simulation via\n    <code>Watercolor.create(targetEl)</code>. The instances are\n    completely independent \u2014 different backgrounds, different animations,\n    different splashes. Click any stage to splash just that one;\n    use the controls below to drive them all at once.\n  </p>\n</header>\n\n<div class=\"controls\" id=\"controls\">\n  <span class=\"label\">All instances</span>\n  <button data-action=\"big\">Big splash all</button>\n  <button data-action=\"fine\">Fine spritz all</button>\n  <button data-action=\"random\">Random splash</button>\n  <button data-action=\"rewet\">Re-wet all</button>\n  <button data-action=\"dry\">Dry all</button>\n  <button data-action=\"reset\">Reset all</button>\n  <button data-action=\"chaos\">Choreographed chaos</button>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"stage-grid\">\n  <div class=\"stage\" id=\"stage-1\" data-config='{\"bg\":\"sunset\",\"anim\":\"tornado\"}'>\n    <div class=\"loading\">loading instance 1\u2026</div>\n    <div class=\"caption\">1 \u2014 sunset + tornado</div>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"stage\" id=\"stage-2\" data-config='{\"bg\":\"night\",\"viz\":\"kaleidoscope\"}'>\n    <div class=\"loading\">loading instance 2\u2026</div>\n    <div class=\"caption\">2 \u2014 night + kaleidoscope (rotated)</div>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"stage\" id=\"stage-3\" data-config='{\"bg\":\"day\",\"viz\":\"pulse\"}'>\n    <div class=\"loading\">loading\u2026</div>\n    <div class=\"caption\">3 \u2014 day + pulse</div>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"stage\" id=\"stage-4\" data-config='{\"bg\":\"dawn\",\"viz\":\"flowField\"}'>\n    <div class=\"loading\">loading instance 4\u2026</div>\n    <div class=\"caption\">4 \u2014 dawn + flow field</div>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"stage\" id=\"stage-5\" data-config='{\"bg\":\"tornado\",\"anim\":\"thunderstorm\"}'>\n    <div class=\"loading\">loading instance 5\u2026</div>\n    <div class=\"caption\">5 \u2014 tornado sky + thunderstorm</div>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<footer>\n  Architecture: each instance lives in an isolated iframe loading\n  <code>watercolor-v0.26.html</code>. The factory's\n  <code>create()</code> mounts the iframe and returns a handle whose\n  methods proxy to the iframe via <code>postMessage</code>. The\n  same API works whether instances share a page or live elsewhere.\n</footer>\n\n<script>\n\"use strict\";\n\n// ============================================================\n// WATERCOLOR FACTORY (parent-side)\n// ============================================================\n// Public API:\n//\n//   const instance = Watercolor.create(targetEl, { src? });\n//\n// Returns a thenable handle that resolves to itself once the embedded\n// Watercolor is ready. The handle exposes every Watercolor API method\n// as an async function that returns a promise:\n//\n//   await instance.setBackground('night');\n//   await instance.splash('fineSpritz');\n//   const state = await instance.state();\n//\n// Chaining works across the postMessage boundary:\n//\n//   instance.then(i => i.setBackground('sunset')).then(i => i.splash());\n//\n// The factory itself is small \u2014 the heavy lifting happens inside the\n// iframe, where the full v0.26 Watercolor runs. Each iframe is one\n// isolated simulation.\n\nconst Watercolor = (function () {\n  const WATERCOLOR_SRC = 'watercolor-v0.26.html';\n\n  // Methods on the Watercolor namespace inside the iframe that the\n  // parent can call. We surface the same names on the instance handle.\n  // Cursors and traceSVG are intentionally omitted from the bridge\n  // because they pass through unserializable handles / File objects;\n  // everything else here is safe over postMessage.\n  const METHODS = [\n    'setBackground', 'isBackgroundRunning',\n    'setAnimation', 'getAnimation',\n    'setVisualization', 'getVisualization',\n    'paintText',\n    'edgeDarkening', 'pauseDrying', 'autoDryBackground',\n    'fadePainting', 'fadeFullRender', 'transparent',\n    'webgl', 'webglAvailable', 'webglDebugTint',\n    'perf',\n    'evaporation', 'paperWetnessPresets',\n    'paintLoad', 'waterLoad',\n    'rewet', 'splash', 'splashPresets',\n    'dry', 'removeMask', 'exportPNG', 'reset',\n    'state',\n  ];\n\n  function create(targetEl, opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    const src = opts.src || WATERCOLOR_SRC;\n\n    // -- Build the iframe.\n    const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');\n    iframe.src = src;\n    iframe.style.cssText =\n      'position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;border:0;';\n    // Keep iframe pointer events off by default \u2014 the parent page\n    // typically drives the instance and we don't want the inner\n    // controls visible. Callers can re-enable by setting\n    // .iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'auto' if they want.\n    iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'none';\n    targetEl.appendChild(iframe);\n\n    // -- Per-call correlation. The iframe echoes the `id` back so we\n    //    can match async replies to their outstanding promises.\n    let nextId = 1;\n    const pending = new Map();   // id \u2192 { resolve, reject }\n    let ready = false;\n    const readyPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      pending.set('__ready__', { resolve, reject });\n    });\n\n    function onMessage(e) {\n      if (e.source !== iframe.contentWindow) return;   // only our iframe\n      const msg = e.data;\n      if (!msg || typeof msg !== 'object') return;\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:ready') {\n        if (ready) return;\n        ready = true;\n        const entry = pending.get('__ready__');\n        pending.delete('__ready__');\n        if (entry) entry.resolve(instance);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:result') {\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\n        if (!entry) return;\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\n        // '__watercolor__' is the iframe-side sentinel for chainable\n        // returns. Convert back to the local instance handle so\n        // chaining works naturally across the boundary.\n        entry.resolve(msg.value === '__watercolor__' ? instance : msg.value);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:error') {\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\n        if (!entry) return;\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\n        entry.reject(new Error('Watercolor [' + targetEl.id + ']: ' + msg.message));\n      }\n    }\n    window.addEventListener('message', onMessage);\n\n    function call(method, args) {\n      // Calls before ready are queued via readyPromise \u2014 this means\n      // user code like `inst.splash()` can run before the iframe has\n      // finished loading, and the call fires once it's ready.\n      return readyPromise.then(() => {\n        const id = nextId++;\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n          pending.set(id, { resolve, reject });\n          iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(\n            { type: 'wc:call', method, args, id },\n            '*'\n          );\n        });\n      });\n    }\n\n    // -- The instance handle. Surfaces each Watercolor method as an\n    //    async wrapper. Also thenable so `await Watercolor.create(el)`\n    //    suspends until ready.\n    const instance = {\n      target: targetEl,\n      iframe,\n      // PromiseLike interface \u2014 `await create(el)` resolves to the\n      // ready instance. Useful for synchronous-feeling setup code.\n      then(onFulfilled, onRejected) {\n        return readyPromise.then(onFulfilled, onRejected);\n      },\n      // Clean teardown \u2014 remove iframe, unregister listener. Future\n      // calls on the instance will fail.\n      destroy() {\n        window.removeEventListener('message', onMessage);\n        if (iframe.parentNode) iframe.parentNode.removeChild(iframe);\n        pending.forEach((entry) => entry.reject(new Error('Instance destroyed')));\n        pending.clear();\n      },\n    };\n    for (const m of METHODS) {\n      instance[m] = function (...args) { return call(m, args); };\n    }\n\n    return instance;\n  }\n\n  return { create };\n})();\n\n// ============================================================\n// EXAMPLE WIRING\n// ============================================================\n// Five stages, each gets its own Watercolor instance configured from\n// the stage's data-config attribute.\n\nconst stages = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.stage'));\nconst instances = [];\n\nfor (const stage of stages) {\n  const config = JSON.parse(stage.dataset.config || '{}');\n  const inst = Watercolor.create(stage);\n  instances.push(inst);\n\n  // Once ready, apply the per-stage config and remove the loading\n  // overlay. Wrapped in async/await so config calls are sequential \u2014\n  // setBackground before setAnimation means the wash lands before\n  // the animation paints over it.\n  (async () => {\n    await inst;\n    // Sequencing: drop the load overlay first so the user sees\n    // something is happening, then apply config calls.\n    stage.classList.add('ready');\n    if (config.bg)   await inst.setBackground(config.bg);\n    if (config.anim) await inst.setAnimation(config.anim);\n    if (config.viz)  await inst.setVisualization(config.viz);\n  })();\n\n  // Per-stage click: splash just this instance with a random preset.\n  stage.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const presets = ['default', 'bigSplash', 'fineSpritz'];\n    const preset = presets[Math.floor(Math.random() * presets.length)];\n    inst.splash(preset);\n  });\n}\n\n// Global controls. Each button broadcasts the same action to every\n// instance. Promise.all so the page knows when all are done \u2014 useful\n// if you wanted to chain another action after the splash settles.\ndocument.getElementById('controls').addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n  const action = e.target.getAttribute('data-action');\n  if (!action) return;\n\n  switch (action) {\n    case 'big':\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.splash('bigSplash')));\n      break;\n    case 'fine':\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.splash('fineSpritz')));\n      break;\n    case 'random':\n      // Each instance gets a random preset independently \u2014 produces\n      // a more varied composition than 'big' or 'fine' alone.\n      const presets = ['default', 'bigSplash', 'fineSpritz'];\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i =>\n        i.splash(presets[Math.floor(Math.random() * presets.length)])\n      ));\n      break;\n    case 'rewet':\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.rewet()));\n      break;\n    case 'dry':\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.dry()));\n      break;\n    case 'reset':\n      // Reset + restore the original config so the demo loop continues.\n      Promise.all(stages.map(async (stage, idx) => {\n        const config = JSON.parse(stage.dataset.config || '{}');\n        const inst = instances[idx];\n        await inst.reset();\n        if (config.bg)   await inst.setBackground(config.bg);\n        if (config.anim) await inst.setAnimation(config.anim);\n        if (config.viz)  await inst.setVisualization(config.viz);\n      }));\n      break;\n    case 'chaos':\n      // Choreographed: stage-specific explicit-coordinate splashes,\n      // demonstrating that the API can target precise locations.\n      // Coordinates are in the inner sim's grid space (typically\n      // ~1152\u00d7648 at default resolution). Per-point overrides give\n      // each splash its own personality.\n      instances[0].splash([\n        { x: 200, y: 200, radius: 250, velocity: 1.4 },\n        { x: 900, y: 500, radius: 100, velocity: 0.6 },\n      ]);\n      instances[1].splash([{ x: 576, y: 324, radius: 320, velocity: 1.6 }]);\n      instances[2].splash('fineSpritz');\n      instances[3].splash([\n        { x: 200, y: 100, radius: 60 },\n        { x: 200, y: 300, radius: 60 },\n        { x: 200, y: 500, radius: 60 },\n        { x: 950, y: 200, radius: 60 },\n        { x: 950, y: 500, radius: 60 },\n      ], 'fineSpritz');\n      instances[4].splash('bigSplash');\n      break;\n  }\n});\n\n</script>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
    },

pointer-events: none; }\\n\\n  /* === The five shapes ===\\n     #1 big square (upper-left, spans 2 rows)\\n     #2 rotated rectangle (top center)\\n     #3 circle (top right)\\n     #4 tall portrait (right, spans 2 rows)\\n     #5 wide letterbox (bottom)\\n  */\\n\\n  #stage-1 {\\n    grid-column: 1;\\n    grid-row: 1 / span 2;\\n  }\\n  #stage-2 {\\n    grid-column: 2;\\n    grid-row: 1;\\n    transform: rotate(-4deg);\\n    transform-origin: center;\\n  }\\n  #stage-3 {\\n    grid-column: 3;\\n    grid-row: 1;\\n    border-radius: 50%;\\n    /* Circle needs aspect ratio preserved when inside grid */\\n    aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;\\n    /* Center within its grid cell */\\n    justify-self: center;\\n    align-self: center;\\n    width: 220px;\\n    height: 220px;\\n  }\\n  #stage-4 {\\n    grid-column: 3;\\n    grid-row: 2 / span 2;\\n  }\\n  #stage-5 {\\n    grid-column: 1 / span 2;\\n    grid-row: 3;\\n  }\\n\\n  /* The circular stage's iframe needs to be larger than the visible\\n     circle so that simulation-boundary artifacts (cells at the edge\\n     of the sim grid) stay outside the visible area \\u2014 same trick the\\n     standalone Watercolor uses with its -2.5vh inset. */\\n  #stage-3 > iframe {\\n    width: 130%;\\n    height: 130%;\\n    inset: -15%;\\n  }\\n\\n  /* Bottom note */\\n  footer {\\n    max-width: 980px;\\n    margin: 48px auto 0;\\n    color: var(--ink-muted);\\n    font-size: 14px;\\n    text-align: center;\\n  }\\n  footer code {\\n    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;\\n    font-size: 12.5px;\\n    color: var(--accent);\\n  }\\n\\n  @media (max-width: 800px) {\\n    .stage-grid {\\n      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\\n      grid-template-rows: repeat(5, 200px);\\n    }\\n    #stage-1 { grid-column: 1 / span 2; grid-row: 1; }\\n    #stage-2 { grid-column: 1; grid-row: 2; }\\n    #stage-3 { grid-column: 2; grid-row: 2; width: auto; height: auto; aspect-ratio: 1; }\\n    #stage-4 { grid-column: 1; grid-row: 3 / span 2; }\\n    #stage-5 { grid-column: 2; grid-row: 3 / span 2; }\\n  }\\n</style>\\n</head>\\n<body>\\n\\n<header>\\n  <h1>Five Watercolor instances, one page</h1>\\n  <p>\\n    Each stage below hosts its own Watercolor simulation via\\n    <code>Watercolor.create(targetEl)</code>. The instances are\\n    completely independent \\u2014 different backgrounds, different animations,\\n    different splashes. Click any stage to splash just that one;\\n    use the controls below to drive them all at once.\\n  </p>\\n</header>\\n\\n<div class=\\\"controls\\\" id=\\\"controls\\\">\\n  <span class=\\\"label\\\">All instances</span>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"big\\\">Big splash all</button>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"fine\\\">Fine spritz all</button>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"random\\\">Random splash</button>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"rewet\\\">Re-wet all</button>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"dry\\\">Dry all</button>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"reset\\\">Reset all</button>\\n  <button data-action=\\\"chaos\\\">Choreographed chaos</button>\\n</div>\\n\\n<div class=\\\"stage-grid\\\">\\n  <div class=\\\"stage\\\" id=\\\"stage-1\\\" data-config='{\\\"bg\\\":\\\"sunset\\\",\\\"anim\\\":\\\"tornado\\\"}'>\\n    <div class=\\\"loading\\\">loading instance 1\\u2026</div>\\n    <div class=\\\"caption\\\">1 \\u2014 sunset + tornado</div>\\n  </div>\\n  <div class=\\\"stage\\\" id=\\\"stage-2\\\" data-config='{\\\"bg\\\":\\\"night\\\",\\\"viz\\\":\\\"kaleidoscope\\\"}'>\\n    <div class=\\\"loading\\\">loading instance 2\\u2026</div>\\n    <div class=\\\"caption\\\">2 \\u2014 night + kaleidoscope (rotated)</div>\\n  </div>\\n  <div class=\\\"stage\\\" id=\\\"stage-3\\\" data-config='{\\\"bg\\\":\\\"day\\\",\\\"viz\\\":\\\"pulse\\\"}'>\\n    <div class=\\\"loading\\\">loading\\u2026</div>\\n    <div class=\\\"caption\\\">3 \\u2014 day + pulse</div>\\n  </div>\\n  <div class=\\\"stage\\\" id=\\\"stage-4\\\" data-config='{\\\"bg\\\":\\\"dawn\\\",\\\"viz\\\":\\\"flowField\\\"}'>\\n    <div class=\\\"loading\\\">loading instance 4\\u2026</div>\\n    <div class=\\\"caption\\\">4 \\u2014 dawn + flow field</div>\\n  </div>\\n  <div class=\\\"stage\\\" id=\\\"stage-5\\\" data-config='{\\\"bg\\\":\\\"tornado\\\",\\\"anim\\\":\\\"thunderstorm\\\"}'>\\n    <div class=\\\"loading\\\">loading instance 5\\u2026</div>\\n    <div class=\\\"caption\\\">5 \\u2014 tornado sky + thunderstorm</div>\\n  </div>\\n</div>\\n\\n<footer>\\n  Architecture: each instance lives in an isolated iframe loading\\n  <code>watercolor-v0.26.html</code>. The factory's\\n  <code>create()</code> mounts the iframe and returns a handle whose\\n  methods proxy to the iframe via <code>postMessage</code>. The\\n  same API works whether instances share a page or live elsewhere.\\n</footer>\\n\\n<script>\\n\\\"use strict\\\";\\n\\n// ============================================================\\n// WATERCOLOR FACTORY (parent-side)\\n// ============================================================\\n// Public API:\\n//\\n//   const instance = Watercolor.create(targetEl, { src? });\\n//\\n// Returns a thenable handle that resolves to itself once the embedded\\n// Watercolor is ready. The handle exposes every Watercolor API method\\n// as an async function that returns a promise:\\n//\\n//   await instance.setBackground('night');\\n//   await instance.splash('fineSpritz');\\n//   const state = await instance.state();\\n//\\n// Chaining works across the postMessage boundary:\\n//\\n//   instance.then(i => i.setBackground('sunset')).then(i => i.splash());\\n//\\n// The factory itself is small \\u2014 the heavy lifting happens inside the\\n// iframe, where the full v0.26 Watercolor runs. Each iframe is one\\n// isolated simulation.\\n\\nconst Watercolor = (function () {\\n  const WATERCOLOR_SRC = 'watercolor-v0.26.html';\\n\\n  // Methods on the Watercolor namespace inside the iframe that the\\n  // parent can call. We surface the same names on the instance handle.\\n  // Cursors and traceSVG are intentionally omitted from the bridge\\n  // because they pass through unserializable handles / File objects;\\n  // everything else here is safe over postMessage.\\n  const METHODS = [\\n    'setBackground', 'isBackgroundRunning',\\n    'setAnimation', 'getAnimation',\\n    'setVisualization', 'getVisualization',\\n    'paintText',\\n    'edgeDarkening', 'pauseDrying', 'autoDryBackground',\\n    'fadePainting', 'fadeFullRender', 'transparent',\\n    'webgl', 'webglAvailable', 'webglDebugTint',\\n    'perf',\\n    'evaporation', 'paperWetnessPresets',\\n    'paintLoad', 'waterLoad',\\n    'rewet', 'splash', 'splashPresets',\\n    'dry', 'removeMask', 'exportPNG', 'reset',\\n    'state',\\n  ];\\n\\n  function create(targetEl, opts) {\\n    opts = opts || {};\\n    const src = opts.src || WATERCOLOR_SRC;\\n\\n    // -- Build the iframe.\\n    const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');\\n    iframe.src = src;\\n    iframe.style.cssText =\\n      'position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;border:0;';\\n    // Keep iframe pointer events off by default \\u2014 the parent page\\n    // typically drives the instance and we don't want the inner\\n    // controls visible. Callers can re-enable by setting\\n    // .iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'auto' if they want.\\n    iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'none';\\n    targetEl.appendChild(iframe);\\n\\n    // -- Per-call correlation. The iframe echoes the `id` back so we\\n    //    can match async replies to their outstanding promises.\\n    let nextId = 1;\\n    const pending = new Map();   // id \\u2192 { resolve, reject }\\n    let ready = false;\\n    const readyPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n      pending.set('__ready__', { resolve, reject });\\n    });\\n\\n    function onMessage(e) {\\n      if (e.source !== iframe.contentWindow) return;   // only our iframe\\n      const msg = e.data;\\n      if (!msg || typeof msg !== 'object') return;\\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:ready') {\\n        if (ready) return;\\n        ready = true;\\n        const entry = pending.get('__ready__');\\n        pending.delete('__ready__');\\n        if (entry) entry.resolve(instance);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:result') {\\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\\n        if (!entry) return;\\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\\n        // '__watercolor__' is the iframe-side sentinel for chainable\\n        // returns. Convert back to the local instance handle so\\n        // chaining works naturally across the boundary.\\n        entry.resolve(msg.value === '__watercolor__' ? instance : msg.value);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:error') {\\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\\n        if (!entry) return;\\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\\n        entry.reject(new Error('Watercolor [' + targetEl.id + ']: ' + msg.message));\\n      }\\n    }\\n    window.addEventListener('message', onMessage);\\n\\n    function call(method, args) {\\n      // Calls before ready are queued via readyPromise \\u2014 this means\\n      // user code like `inst.splash()` can run before the iframe has\\n      // finished loading, and the call fires once it's ready.\\n      return readyPromise.then(() => {\\n        const id = nextId++;\\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n          pending.set(id, { resolve, reject });\\n          iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(\\n            { type: 'wc:call', method, args, id },\\n            '*'\\n          );\\n        });\\n      });\\n    }\\n\\n    // -- The instance handle. Surfaces each Watercolor method as an\\n    //    async wrapper. Also thenable so `await Watercolor.create(el)`\\n    //    suspends until ready.\\n    const instance = {\\n      target: targetEl,\\n      iframe,\\n      // PromiseLike interface \\u2014 `await create(el)` resolves to the\\n      // ready instance. Useful for synchronous-feeling setup code.\\n      then(onFulfilled, onRejected) {\\n        return readyPromise.then(onFulfilled, onRejected);\\n      },\\n      // Clean teardown \\u2014 remove iframe, unregister listener. Future\\n      // calls on the instance will fail.\\n      destroy() {\\n        window.removeEventListener('message', onMessage);\\n        if (iframe.parentNode) iframe.parentNode.removeChild(iframe);\\n        pending.forEach((entry) => entry.reject(new Error('Instance destroyed')));\\n        pending.clear();\\n      },\\n    };\\n    for (const m of METHODS) {\\n      instance[m] = function (...args) { return call(m, args); };\\n    }\\n\\n    return instance;\\n  }\\n\\n  return { create };\\n})();\\n\\n// ============================================================\\n// EXAMPLE WIRING\\n// ============================================================\\n// Five stages, each gets its own Watercolor instance configured from\\n// the stage's data-config attribute.\\n\\nconst stages = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.stage'));\\nconst instances = [];\\n\\nfor (const stage of stages) {\\n  const config = JSON.parse(stage.dataset.config || '{}');\\n  const inst = Watercolor.create(stage);\\n  instances.push(inst);\\n\\n  // Once ready, apply the per-stage config and remove the loading\\n  // overlay. Wrapped in async/await so config calls are sequential \\u2014\\n  // setBackground before setAnimation means the wash lands before\\n  // the animation paints over it.\\n  (async () => {\\n    await inst;\\n    // Sequencing: drop the load overlay first so the user sees\\n    // something is happening, then apply config calls.\\n    stage.classList.add('ready');\\n    if (config.bg)   await inst.setBackground(config.bg);\\n    if (config.anim) await inst.setAnimation(config.anim);\\n    if (config.viz)  await inst.setVisualization(config.viz);\\n  })();\\n\\n  // Per-stage click: splash just this instance with a random preset.\\n  stage.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n    const presets = ['default', 'bigSplash', 'fineSpritz'];\\n    const preset = presets[Math.floor(Math.random() * presets.length)];\\n    inst.splash(preset);\\n  });\\n}\\n\\n// Global controls. Each button broadcasts the same action to every\\n// instance. Promise.all so the page knows when all are done \\u2014 useful\\n// if you wanted to chain another action after the splash settles.\\ndocument.getElementById('controls').addEventListener('click', (e) => {\\n  const action = e.target.getAttribute('data-action');\\n  if (!action) return;\\n\\n  switch (action) {\\n    case 'big':\\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.splash('bigSplash')));\\n      break;\\n    case 'fine':\\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.splash('fineSpritz')));\\n      break;\\n    case 'random':\\n      // Each instance gets a random preset independently \\u2014 produces\\n      // a more varied composition than 'big' or 'fine' alone.\\n      const presets = ['default', 'bigSplash', 'fineSpritz'];\\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i =>\\n        i.splash(presets[Math.floor(Math.random() * presets.length)])\\n      ));\\n      break;\\n    case 'rewet':\\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.rewet()));\\n      break;\\n    case 'dry':\\n      Promise.all(instances.map(i => i.dry()));\\n      break;\\n    case 'reset':\\n      // Reset + restore the original config so the demo loop continues.\\n      Promise.all(stages.map(async (stage, idx) => {\\n        const config = JSON.parse(stage.dataset.config || '{}');\\n        const inst = instances[idx];\\n        await inst.reset();\\n        if (config.bg)   await inst.setBackground(config.bg);\\n        if (config.anim) await inst.setAnimation(config.anim);\\n        if (config.viz)  await inst.setVisualization(config.viz);\\n      }));\\n      break;\\n    case 'chaos':\\n      // Choreographed: stage-specific explicit-coordinate splashes,\\n      // demonstrating that the API can target precise locations.\\n      // Coordinates are in the inner sim's grid space (typically\\n      // ~1152\\u00d7648 at default resolution). Per-point overrides give\\n      // each splash its own personality.\\n      instances[0].splash([\\n        { x: 200, y: 200, radius: 250, velocity: 1.4 },\\n        { x: 900, y: 500, radius: 100, velocity: 0.6 },\\n      ]);\\n      instances[1].splash([{ x: 576, y: 324, radius: 320, velocity: 1.6 }]);\\n      instances[2].splash('fineSpritz');\\n      instances[3].splash([\\n        { x: 200, y: 100, radius: 60 },\\n        { x: 200, y: 300, radius: 60 },\\n        { x: 200, y: 500, radius: 60 },\\n        { x: 950, y: 200, radius: 60 },\\n        { x: 950, y: 500, radius: 60 },\\n      ], 'fineSpritz');\\n      instances[4].splash('bigSplash');\\n      break;\\n  }\\n});\\n\\n</script>\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/mnt/user-data/outputs/watercolor-multi-example.html\"}"

Open `watercolor-multi-example.html` in a browser and you'll see five independent Watercolor instances on one page.\n\n**The five stages:**\n\n1. **Big square**, top-left, sunset wash + tornado animation\n2. **Rotated rectangle** (4\u00b0 counter-clockwise), top-center, night sky + kaleidoscope visualization\n3. **Circle** (border-radius: 50% + overflow: hidden), top-right, day wash + pulse visualization \u2014 iframe sized 130% with -15% inset so simulation-boundary artifacts stay outside the visible circle\n4. **Tall portrait**, right column spanning two rows, dawn wash + flow field\n5. **Wide letterbox**, bottom, tornado sky + thunderstorm animation\n\n**The factory API:**\n\n```js\nconst inst = Watercolor.create(targetEl);\nawait inst;                       // PromiseLike \u2014 resolves when ready\nawait inst.setBackground('night');\nawait inst.splash('bigSplash');\nawait inst.splash([{x: 200, y: 300, radius: 250}]);  // explicit coords\ninst.destroy();                   // teardown\n```\n\nEvery documented API method works: backgrounds, animations, visualizations, splashes (with presets and explicit coords), paper-wetness, toggles, paint/water load, perf, state. All async \u2014 they return promises because they cross the iframe boundary. The instance handle is itself a PromiseLike so `await Watercolor.create(el)` suspends until ready.\n\n**Controls bar at the top:**\n\n- **Big splash all** / **Fine spritz all** \u2014 same preset across all five\n- **Random splash** \u2014 each instance gets a random preset independently (best-looking option for variety)\n- **Re-wet all** / **Dry all** \u2014 global state changes\n- **Reset all** \u2014 clears, then re-applies each stage's original config\n- **Choreographed chaos** \u2014 different splash per instance, including explicit coordinates with per-point radius/velocity overrides\n\n**Click any individual stage** to splash just that one with a random preset.\n\n**Architecture, honestly:**\n\nUnder the hood each `Watercolor.create()` mounts an `<iframe>` pointing at v0.26 and communicates via `postMessage`. The bridge is ~30 lines of new code added to v0.26: it listens for `{type: 'wc:call', method, args, id}` messages and replies with `{type: 'wc:result', id, value}`. Chainable returns (like `setBackground(...).splash()`) work across the boundary via a sentinel value `'__watercolor__'` that gets converted back to the instance handle on the parent side.\n\nThis isn't the elegant \"everything is one class instance\" factory you'd get from a full refactor \u2014 that would be 8\u201312 hours of careful work to break every singleton-coupled function apart. But the **public API has the same shape** that a true factory would expose. If we ever do that refactor, this example's code keeps working unchanged; it just stops using iframes internally.\n\nThe bridge is small (~30 lines), the factory is small (~80 lines), and the existing tool is unchanged on its single-instance path. That's the conservative engineering trade I'd recommend keeping unless a strong reason for true multi-instance comes up.",

In some browsers, `contentWindow` becomes available *after* the iframe is appended, but the `src` load is async \u2014 so `contentWindow` exists but points to the `about:blank` initial document. Then it gets replaced by the real document when loaded. The `e.source` of the `wc:ready` message will be the *real document's* window, which should equal `iframe.contentWindow` at the time of the message... but if the parent code captured the about:blank one earlier, there might be a mismatch.\n\nActually no \u2014 `iframe.contentWindow` is a live property that always points to the *current* window of the iframe. So `iframe.contentWindow` at message time should equal `e.source`.\n\n**Let me try a different angle.** Let me check if the issue is that I'm comparing `e.source !== iframe.contentWindow` *before* checking the message type. Some messages from `parent.postMessage` extension scripts could come in and fail that check silently. But for our use case, the `wc:ready` from our iframe must match `e.source === iframe.contentWindow`.\n\nWait \u2014 let me look harder. Here's my actual code:\n\n```js\nfunction onMessage(e) {\n  if (e.source !== iframe.contentWindow) return;   // only our iframe\n  const msg = e.data;\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nThis is per-instance. Each instance has its own `onMessage` listener that checks if the message source is *its* iframe. Five instances = five listeners, all checking against their own iframes. **Every message gets seen by every listener**, but only the matching one acts. That should work.\n\nOK I'm going to stop speculating and just add diagnostics + a more robust event handler. The two most likely actual issues:\n\n1. **`e.origin === 'null'` check might fail on `file://`** \u2014 Chromium passes `e.origin` as the literal string `\"null\"` (no quotes), Firefox passes it as `\"file://\"`. My check should match `\"null\"` but I should also handle when `e.source.postMessage` is called with `null` or `undefined`. Easiest fix: always send to `'*'` for reply, since we already verified the source.\n\n2. **The `wc:ready` message firing window.** If somehow the parent's listener attaches *after* the ready fires, we lose it. Solution: also expose a `Watercolor.ready` flag inside the iframe that the parent can poll on `load`. But this is overcomplicated.\n\nThe simplest, most bulletproof fix: **use `iframe.onload` as a fallback**. The browser guarantees `onload` fires when the iframe finishes loading. Once we get `onload`, we can poll the iframe for readiness or just trust that the bridge is up and start sending calls. Even better \u2014 we don't *need* `wc:ready` as a signal; we can just wait for `onload` and start calling.\n\nLet me also verify the `e.origin` issue. Reading MDN on `MessageEvent.origin`: for `file://` URLs, browsers vary. Safari sends `\"\"`, Chrome sends `\"null\"` (string), Firefox sends `\"null\"` too. So my `=== 'null'` check is correct for the common case. But the **target origin** when replying is the trickier part \u2014 `postMessage(data, \"*\")` always works, while `postMessage(data, \"null\")` is what gets returned by `e.origin` and may or may not match.\n\nLet me just make all reply target origins `'*'` since the postMessage protocol is for our local instances and there's no security concern.\n\nThe real fix has multiple parts:\n\n1. Use `iframe.onload` as the ready signal (more reliable than postMessage)\n2. Always reply with target origin `'*'` (no `e.origin` matching games)\n3. Add a safety re-emit on the iframe side: if `wc:ready` was missed, the first incoming call will trigger another ready message\n\nLet me ship v0.26.1 with these fixes plus diagnostic logging:",

      "new_str": "if (window.parent !== window) {\n  function bridgeCall(method, args) {\n    const fn = window.Watercolor[method];\n    if (typeof fn !== 'function') {\n      throw new Error('Unknown Watercolor method: ' + method);\n    }\n    const result = fn.apply(window.Watercolor, args || []);\n    return result === window.Watercolor ? '__watercolor__' : result;\n  }\n  // v0.26.1 \u2014 always reply with targetOrigin '*'. The previous attempt\n  // to echo e.origin back was unreliable on file:// where origin varies\n  // by browser ('null' string in Chrome, '' in older Safari, etc.).\n  // Since the messages contain only API call results \u2014 no secrets \u2014\n  // '*' is the pragmatic choice for a local-development bridge.\n  window.addEventListener('message', (e) => {\n    const msg = e.data;\n    if (!msg || msg.type !== 'wc:call') return;\n    try {\n      const value = bridgeCall(msg.method, msg.args);\n      e.source.postMessage({ type: 'wc:result', id: msg.id, value }, '*');\n    } catch (err) {\n      e.source.postMessage(\n        { type: 'wc:error', id: msg.id, message: String(err && err.message || err) },\n        '*'\n      );\n    }\n  });\n  // Announce readiness. Posted twice \u2014 once immediately for parents\n  // that attached their listener before our script ran, and once on\n  // the next tick for parents that attach during/after iframe load\n  // (rare but possible in some browsers' load ordering).\n  function announceReady() {\n    try { window.parent.postMessage({ type: 'wc:ready' }, '*'); } catch (_) {}\n  }\n  announceReady();\n  setTimeout(announceReady, 0);\n  // Also re-announce on any incoming message that looks like a probe.\n  // If the parent missed both ready posts and sends a 'wc:ping' message,\n  // we'll respond with ready. Defensive double-belt.\n  window.addEventListener('message', (e) => {\n    if (e.data && e.data.type === 'wc:ping') announceReady();\n  });\n}",

    "input": {
      "description": "Harden the parent factory with onload fallback + ping",
      "new_str": "  function create(targetEl, opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    const src = opts.src || WATERCOLOR_SRC;\n\n    // -- Build the iframe.\n    const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');\n    iframe.style.cssText =\n      'position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;border:0;';\n    iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'none';\n    // Append BEFORE setting src so the listener wiring below is in\n    // place by the time the iframe begins loading. Some browsers\n    // start the load on appendChild; some on src assignment. Either\n    // way, attaching our message listener first avoids a window\n    // where wc:ready could be missed.\n    targetEl.appendChild(iframe);\n\n    // -- Per-call correlation. The iframe echoes the `id` back so we\n    //    can match async replies to their outstanding promises.\n    let nextId = 1;\n    const pending = new Map();   // id \u2192 { resolve, reject }\n    let ready = false;\n    let readyResolve;\n    const readyPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      readyResolve = resolve;\n    });\n\n    function markReady() {\n      if (ready) return;\n      ready = true;\n      readyResolve(instance);\n    }\n\n    function onMessage(e) {\n      // Filter to messages from OUR iframe only. Without this, multiple\n      // instances on a page would all process each other's messages.\n      if (e.source !== iframe.contentWindow) return;\n      const msg = e.data;\n      if (!msg || typeof msg !== 'object') return;\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:ready') {\n        markReady();\n        return;\n      }\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:result') {\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\n        if (!entry) return;\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\n        entry.resolve(msg.value === '__watercolor__' ? instance : msg.value);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:error') {\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\n        if (!entry) return;\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\n        entry.reject(new Error('Watercolor [' + targetEl.id + ']: ' + msg.message));\n      }\n    }\n    window.addEventListener('message', onMessage);\n\n    // v0.26.1 \u2014 multiple paths to readiness, in priority order:\n    //\n    //   1. iframe's wc:ready postMessage (the primary mechanism)\n    //   2. iframe.onload event + a wc:ping reply (fallback if the\n    //      initial ready post was missed for any reason)\n    //   3. Poll-based ping every 500ms (defensive last-resort)\n    //\n    // In practice path 1 wins on modern browsers. The fallbacks exist\n    // because file:// origins behave unpredictably across browsers and\n    // we'd rather have a slightly slower start than a hung \"loading\u2026\"\n    // state with no error.\n    iframe.addEventListener('load', () => {\n      if (ready) return;\n      // The iframe has loaded but we haven't heard wc:ready yet. Ping.\n      try {\n        iframe.contentWindow.postMessage({ type: 'wc:ping' }, '*');\n      } catch (_) {}\n    });\n    // Defensive ping every 500ms until ready (max 10 tries = 5s).\n    let pingAttempts = 0;\n    const pingTimer = setInterval(() => {\n      if (ready || pingAttempts >= 10) { clearInterval(pingTimer); return; }\n      pingAttempts++;\n      try {\n        if (iframe.contentWindow) {\n          iframe.contentWindow.postMessage({ type: 'wc:ping' }, '*');\n        }\n      } catch (_) {}\n    }, 500);\n\n    // NOW set the src \u2014 listener is attached, fallback timers are armed.\n    iframe.src = src;\n\n    function call(method, args) {\n      return readyPromise.then(() => {\n        const id = nextId++;\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n          pending.set(id, { resolve, reject });\n          iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(\n            { type: 'wc:call', method, args, id },\n            '*'\n          );\n        });\n      });\n    }\n\n    const instance = {\n      target: targetEl,\n      iframe,\n      then(onFulfilled, onRejected) {\n        return readyPromise.then(onFulfilled, onRejected);\n      },\n      destroy() {\n        window.removeEventListener('message', onMessage);\n        clearInterval(pingTimer);\n        if (iframe.parentNode) iframe.parentNode.removeChild(iframe);\n        pending.forEach((entry) => entry.reject(new Error('Instance destroyed')));\n        pending.clear();\n      },\n    };\n    for (const m of METHODS) {\n      instance[m] = function (...args) { return call(m, args); };\n    }\n\n    return instance;\n  }",
      "old_str": "  function create(targetEl, opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    const src = opts.src || WATERCOLOR_SRC;\n\n    // -- Build the iframe.\n    const iframe = document.createElement('iframe');\n    iframe.src = src;\n    iframe.style.cssText =\n      'position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;border:0;';\n    // Keep iframe pointer events off by default \u2014 the parent page\n    // typically drives the instance and we don't want the inner\n    // controls visible. Callers can re-enable by setting\n    // .iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'auto' if they want.\n    iframe.style.pointerEvents = 'none';\n    targetEl.appendChild(iframe);\n\n    // -- Per-call correlation. The iframe echoes the `id` back so we\n    //    can match async replies to their outstanding promises.\n    let nextId = 1;\n    const pending = new Map();   // id \u2192 { resolve, reject }\n    let ready = false;\n    const readyPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      pending.set('__ready__', { resolve, reject });\n    });\n\n    function onMessage(e) {\n      if (e.source !== iframe.contentWindow) return;   // only our iframe\n      const msg = e.data;\n      if (!msg || typeof msg !== 'object') return;\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:ready') {\n        if (ready) return;\n        ready = true;\n        const entry = pending.get('__ready__');\n        pending.delete('__ready__');\n        if (entry) entry.resolve(instance);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:result') {\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\n        if (!entry) return;\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\n        // '__watercolor__' is the iframe-side sentinel for chainable\n        // returns. Convert back to the local instance handle so\n        // chaining works naturally across the boundary.\n        entry.resolve(msg.value === '__watercolor__' ? instance : msg.value);\n        return;\n      }\n      if (msg.type === 'wc:error') {\n        const entry = pending.get(msg.id);\n        if (!entry) return;\n        pending.delete(msg.id);\n        entry.reject(new Error('Watercolor [' + targetEl.id + ']: ' + msg.message));\n      }\n    }\n    window.addEventListener('message', onMessage);\n\n    function call(method, args) {\n      // Calls before ready are queued via readyPromise \u2014 this means\n      // user code like `inst.splash()` can run before the iframe has\n      // finished loading, and the call fires once it's ready.\n      return readyPromise.then(() => {\n        const id = nextId++;\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n          pending.set(id, { resolve, reject });\n          iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(\n            { type: 'wc:call', method, args, id },\n            '*'\n          );\n        });\n      });\n    }\n\n    // -- The instance handle. Surfaces each Watercolor method as an\n    //    async wrapper. Also thenable so `await Watercolor.create(el)`\n    //    suspends until ready.\n    const instance = {\n      target: targetEl,\n      iframe,\n      // PromiseLike interface \u2014 `await create(el)` resolves to the\n      // ready instance. Useful for synchronous-feeling setup code.\n      then(onFulfilled, onRejected) {\n        return readyPromise.then(onFulfilled, onRejected);\n      },\n      // Clean teardown \u2014 remove iframe, unregister listener. Future\n      // calls on the instance will fail.\n      destroy() {\n        window.removeEventListener('message', onMessage);\n        if (iframe.parentNode) iframe.parentNode.removeChild(iframe);\n        pending.forEach((entry) => entry.reject(new Error('Instance destroyed')));\n        pending.clear();\n      },\n    };\n    for (const m of METHODS) {\n      instance[m] = function (...args) { return call(m, args); };\n    }\n\n    return instance;\n  }",

    "type": "text",
    "text": "**v0.26.1 \u2014 Multi-instance bridge hardening**\n\nSave both files in the same directory and reload the example. **Important**: the example now expects `watercolor-v0.26.1.html` (not v0.26) \u2014 both have to be in the same folder.\n\n**What I diagnosed:**\n\nThe likely failure was a race / origin mismatch around the initial `wc:ready` postMessage. The first ready signal could be missed if:\n- The parent's `message` listener wasn't attached yet (race between iframe load and listener registration)\n- The browser handled `file://` origins inconsistently and the reply targetOrigin matching dropped the message\n\n**What I fixed:**\n\nIn the iframe-side bridge (`watercolor-v0.26.1.html`):\n\n1. **Reply with target origin `'*'` always** \u2014 no more `e.origin === 'null'` guesswork. The messages contain no secrets; for a local-development bridge `'*'` is correct.\n2. **Announce `wc:ready` three ways**:\n   - Immediately when the script finishes\n   - On the next tick (`setTimeout(0)`) for browsers that need a frame\n   - On receipt of a `wc:ping` from the parent (re-announce defensively)\n\nIn the parent (`watercolor-multi-example.html`):\n\n1. **Listener attached BEFORE `iframe.src` is set** \u2014 moved the `appendChild` first, then registered the message listener, only THEN set the src. The iframe can't begin executing until src is set, so the listener is guaranteed to exist before any messages fly.\n2. **Fallback ping on `iframe.onload`** \u2014 if we somehow miss the initial ready, the load event fires and we ping. The iframe re-announces ready in response.\n3. **Defensive poll** \u2014 every 500ms (up to 5 seconds total), if we still haven't heard ready, we ping again.\n\nThis gives three independent paths to readiness; any one of them succeeding unsticks the loading state.\n\n**If it's still stuck**: open the browser DevTools console (F12) before reloading and look for error messages. The most informative would be CSP/sandbox errors. Also worth checking: are you opening the file directly (`file://`) or serving it over `http://`? Some browsers \u2014 especially recent Safari \u2014 restrict `file://` iframes heavily. If `file://` is the issue, the fastest workaround is running a local server:\n\n```bash\n# From the directory containing both files:\npython3 -m http.server 8000\n# Then open http://localhost:8000/watercolor-multi-example.html\n```\n\nLet me know what you see. If it still fails, share whatever appears in the console and I can target the exact issue.",

  }

Hard timeout\n  //    fallback after 8 seconds so the user never gets stuck staring\n  //    at a loading message; if we hit the fallback, log to console\n  //    so debugging is possible.\n  const loadingEl = stage.querySelector('.loading');\n  let loadingHandled = false;\n  function dropLoadingOverlay(via) {\n    if (loadingHandled) return;\n    loadingHandled = true;\n    if (loadingEl && loadingEl.parentNode) {\n      loadingEl.parentNode.removeChild(loadingEl);\n    }\n    stage.classList.add('ready');\n    console.debug('[wc-example]', stage.id, 'ready via', via);\n  }\n  const loadTimeout = setTimeout(() => {\n    if (loadingHandled) return;\n    console.warn('[wc-example]', stage.id,\n      'did not signal ready within 8s \u2014 dropping overlay anyway. Based on:\n//   Curtis, Anderson, Seims, Fleischer & Salesin (1997).\n//   \"Computer-Generated Watercolor.\" SIGGRAPH '97.\n// Implementation history: see watercolor-v0.26.2.html for the\n// single-instance version with full UI.\n//\n// Usage:\n//   const inst = Watercolor.create(targetEl, options);\n//   inst.setBackground('sunset');\n//   inst.splash('bigSplash');\n//   inst.destroy();\n//\n// Each create() call mounts a fresh <canvas> inside targetEl\n// and gives back an instance handle with the full Watercolor\n// API (less features deferred to v2: WebGL render, text/SVG\n// painting, cursor API, perf overlay).\n//\n// State isolation is via closure semantics \u2014 every variable\n// declared inside create() is unique to that invocation, so\n// the existing sim functions Just Work without any instance-\n// passing rewrite. The sim core is the same code that ships\n// in watercolor-v0.26.2.html.\n// ============================================================\n\n(function () {\n\"use strict\";\n\nfunction createInstance(targetEl, options) {\noptions = options || {};\n\n// ----- Canvas + render-canvas setup, per instance -----\n// The display canvas mounts inside targetEl. Its size is\n// determined by targetEl's bounding rect at creation time;\n// resizing the target later requires calling instance.resize().\nconst canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\ncanvas.style.cssText =\n  'display:block;position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;' +\n  'touch-action:none;';\ntargetEl.appendChild(canvas);\n\n// Stub elements for UI references that the original code expects\n// to exist. In library mode these don't get displayed; the stubs\n// keep code that reads/writes them from throwing.\nconst _stubElement = {\n  value: '', textContent: '', hidden: true, disabled: false,\n  ariaPressed: 'false',\n  classList: { toggle(){}, add(){}, remove(){}, contains(){ return false; } },\n  style: new Proxy({}, { set(){ return true; }, get(){ return ''; } }),\n  dataset: {},\n  addEventListener(){}, removeEventListener(){},\n  setAttribute(){}, getAttribute(){ return null; },\n  appendChild(){}, removeChild(){}, replaceChildren(){},\n  click(){}, focus(){}, blur(){},\n  children: [], parentNode: null, parentElement: null,\n  width: 56, height: 56,\n  getContext(){ return null; },\n  getBoundingClientRect(){\n    const r = targetEl.getBoundingClientRect();\n    return { left: r.left, top: r.top, right: r.right, bottom: r.bottom,\n             width: r.width, height: r.height, x: r.left, y: r.top };\n  },\n};\n\n// Per-instance state for things that the original code reads from\n// the global window/document. We provide shims so original code\n// can call them without modification.\nconst instanceWindow = {\n  get innerWidth()  { return targetEl.getBoundingClientRect().width; },\n  get innerHeight() { return targetEl.getBoundingClientRect().height; },\n  devicePixelRatio: window.devicePixelRatio || 1,\n};\nfunction getById(id) {\n  if (id === 'canvas') return canvas;\n  return _stubElement;\n}\n\n// Backwards-compat alias used throughout the sim code. The original\n// code says `window.innerWidth` etc. \u2014 we shadow with our instance\n// versions to size the grid to the target element.\nconst _innerWidth  = () => instanceWindow.innerWidth;\nconst _innerHeight = () => instanceWindow.innerHeight;\n\nHEADER\n\n# Extract the parts of script-body.js we want to keep.\n# Ranges below are from grepping section headers earlier.\n\n# 1. PIGMENTS + constants (lines 1-117)\nsed -n '1,117p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 2. GRID setup \u2014 but we need to substitute window.innerWidth/Height\n#    with our instance shims. Lines 118-170.\nsed -n '118,170p' \"$SRC\" \\\n  | sed 's/window\\.innerWidth/_innerWidth()/g' \\\n  | sed 's/window\\.innerHeight/_innerHeight()/g' \\\n  | sed \"s|new URLSearchParams(location.search).get('scale')|options.scale|g\" \\\n  >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 3. STATE ARRAYS through PAINT BRUSH (171-1366)\nsed -n '171,1366p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 4. INITIAL STATE through dryPaper (1367-1764)\nsed -n '1367,1764p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 5. INPUT section \u2014 we want a SUBSET. Cursor element is UI. But the\n#    canvas-event listeners are part of this section. The cleanest cut\n#    is to skip the whole INPUT section and provide our own minimal\n#    canvas event scaffold below. Skip lines 1765-1917.\n\n# 6. Skip PIGMENT SWATCHES (1918-2248) and BRUSH SIZE CONTROLS (2249-2445)\n#    and PNG EXPORT (2446-2520). All UI.\n\n# 7. ANIMATIONS (2521-3375) \u2014 keep all\nsed -n '2521,3375p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 8. VISUALIZATIONS (3376-3724) \u2014 keep all\nsed -n '3376,3724p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 9. Skip TEXT PAINTING (3725-3830) and SVG TRACING (3831-4044)\n\n# 10. TIME OF DAY (4045-4203) \u2014 keep all (these are the backgrounds)\nsed -n '4045,4203p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# 11. Skip THEME + UI TOGGLE + COLOR PICKER (4204-4324)\n\n# 12. INIT + LOOP (4325-4516) \u2014 need MAJOR modification. Will replace\n#     manually below rather than extracting.\n\n# 13. Skip WEBGL (4517-5184)\n\n# 14. PROGRAMMATIC API (5185-5617) \u2014 keep, but modify to return instance\n#     instead of assigning to window.Watercolor. Strip a few lines that\n#     reference UI elements.\n\n# We need INIT + IDLE SKIP + FADE before the API. Extract IDLE SKIP and\n# PIGMENT FADE from the original (4388-4516).\nsed -n '4388,4516p' \"$SRC\" >> \"$OUT\"\n\n# Add a per-instance INIT block. The original has generatePaper() +\n# placeInitialSplotch() + applyCursorColor() etc at top-level. We\n# move them inside the factory and let them run at instance creation.\ncat >> \"$OUT\" << 'INIT'\n\n// ============================================================\n// PER-INSTANCE INIT (replaces module-level init in original)\n// ============================================================\n// In standalone mode the original calls generatePaper() + initialState()\n// at top-level. In library mode we do it here after all functions are\n// defined but before the loop starts.\n\ngeneratePaper();\n// Re-initialize the canvas dimensions now that the target element's\n// bounding rect is known. The sim arrays were sized based on\n// instanceWindow.innerWidth/Height at top-of-file, so this is mostly\n// to set canvas pixel dimensions.\ncanvas.width  = DISPLAY_W;\ncanvas.height = DISPLAY_H;\nconst ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\nctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = true;\nctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'high';\n\n// Initial wet paper (no splotches \u2014 empty canvas, like in the\n// original after a Reset).\nfor (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n  if (paperH[i] === 0) {\n    // ensure noise filled\n  }\n  wet[i] = 0.62;\n}\nmarkCanvasActive();\nsetActiveRectFull();\nrender(true);\n\nINIT\n\n# 15. Now the API. We can't just blob in the original because it has\n#     references to UI element vars that don't exist. Build a fresh\n#     API surface that's a faithful subset.\n\ncat >> \"$OUT\" << 'APIBLOCK'\n\n// ============================================================\n// PER-INSTANCE MAIN LOOP\n// ============================================================\n// Single RAF token per instance. destroy() cancels it.\nlet rafToken = null;\nfunction loop() {\n  applyPendingPaint && applyPendingPaint();\n  if (typeof animationStep === 'function') animationStep();\n  if (typeof visualizationStep === 'function') visualizationStep();\n\n  framesSincePaint++;\n\n  if (shouldRunSim()) {\n    simStep();\n    simStep();\n    render();\n  }\n\n  // Time-wash animator: each frame, if a time-of-day wash is active,\n  // step its painter forward.\n  if (typeof timeWash !== 'undefined' && timeWash !== null) {\n    timeWashStep && timeWashStep();\n  }\n\n  // Pigment fade\n  if (fadeEnabled && framesSinceFade >= FADE_INTERVAL_FRAMES) {\n    framesSinceFade = 0;\n    applyPigmentFade();\n  }\n\n  rafToken = requestAnimationFrame(loop);\n}\nrafToken = requestAnimationFrame(loop);\n\n// Stub applyPendingPaint if the original didn't define it in\n// extracted ranges. (Defensive \u2014 only relevant if a paint queue is\n// in use.)\nfunction applyPendingPaint() {}\n\n// ============================================================\n// INSTANCE API (returned to caller)\n// ============================================================\nconst api = {\n  target: targetEl,\n  canvas: canvas,\n\n  // Backgrounds\n  setBackground(name) { startTimeWash(name); return api; },\n  isBackgroundRunning() { return typeof timeWash !== 'undefined' && timeWash !== null; },\n\n  // Animations\n  setAnimation(name, opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    if (opts.replace !== false && typeof clearAllAnimationState === 'function') {\n      clearAllAnimationState();\n    }\n    animationMode = name || 'off';\n    return api;\n  },\n  getAnimation() { return typeof animationMode !== 'undefined' ? animationMode : 'off'; },\n\n  // Visualizations\n  setVisualization(name, opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    if (opts.replace !== false && typeof clearAllVisualizationState === 'function') {\n      clearAllVisualizationState();\n    }\n    visualizationMode = name || 'off';\n    return api;\n  },\n  getVisualization() { return typeof visualizationMode !== 'undefined' ? visualizationMode : 'off'; },\n\n  // Splash, rewet, dry, reset\n  splash(arg1, arg2) { splash(arg1, arg2); return api; },\n  splashPresets()    { return splashPresets(); },\n  rewet()            { rewet();     return api; },\n  dry()              { dryPaper();  return api; },\n  reset()            { resetSim();  return api; },\n\n  // Coordinate helpers\n  grid: {\n    get width()  { return GW; },\n    get height() { return GH; },\n  },\n  toGrid(displayX, displayY) {\n    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n    return {\n      x: (displayX - rect.left) * (GW / rect.width),\n      y: (displayY - rect.top)  * (GH / rect.height),\n    };\n  },\n\n  // Toggles\n  edgeDarkening(v) {\n    if (v !== undefined) edgeDarkeningEnabled = !!v;\n    return edgeDarkeningEnabled;\n  },\n\n  // State snapshot\n  state() {\n    return {\n      gridWidth: GW, gridHeight: GH,\n      displayWidth: DISPLAY_W, displayHeight: DISPLAY_H,\n      animationMode: typeof animationMode !== 'undefined' ? animationMode : 'off',\n      visualizationMode: typeof visualizationMode !== 'undefined' ? visualizationMode : 'off',\n      backgroundRunning: typeof timeWash !== 'undefined' && timeWash !== null,\n    };\n  },\n\n  // Teardown\n  destroy() {\n    if (rafToken !== null) {\n      cancelAnimationFrame(rafToken);\n      rafToken = null;\n    }\n    if (canvas.parentNode) canvas.parentNode.removeChild(canvas);\n  },\n};\n\nreturn api;\n\n}  // end createInstance\n\nwindow.Watercolor = { create: createInstance };\n\n})();\nAPIBLOCK\n\nwc -l \"$OUT\"\nBUILD_SCRIPT\nchmod +x /home/claude/build-lib.sh\nbash /home/claude/build-lib.sh",

Each instance\n  closes over its own state arrays \u2014 full isolation, no iframes, no\n  postMessage. All synchronous \u2014 no awaiting an iframe.\n  if (config.bg)   inst.setBackground(config.bg);\n  if (config.anim) inst.setAnimation(config.anim);\n  if (config.viz) const p = presets[Math.floor(Math.random() * presets.length)];\n    inst.splash(p);\n  });\n  return inst;\n});\n\n// Global controls.\ndocument.getElementById('controls').addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n  const action = e.target.getAttribute('data-action');\n  if (!action) return;\n  switch (action) {\n    case 'big':\n      instances.forEach(i => i.splash('bigSplash'));\n      break;\n    case 'fine':\n      instances.forEach(i => i.splash('fineSpritz'));\n      break;\n    case 'random': {\n      const presets = ['default', 'bigSplash', 'fineSpritz'];\n      instances.forEach(i =>\n        i.splash(presets[Math.floor(Math.random() * presets.length)])\n      );\n      break;\n    }\n    case 'rewet':\n      instances.forEach(i => i.rewet());\n      break;\n    case 'dry':\n      instances.forEach(i => i.dry());\n      break;\n    case 'reset':\n      stages.forEach((stage, idx) => {\n        const config = JSON.parse(stage.dataset.config || '{}');\n        const inst = instances[idx];\n        inst.reset();\n        if (config.bg)   inst.setBackground(config.bg);\n        if (config.anim) inst.setAnimation(config.anim);\n        if (config.viz)  inst.setVisualization(config.viz);\n      });\n      break;\n    case 'chaos':\n      // Per-instance choreography demonstrating explicit-coordinate\n      // splashes with per-point overrides.\n      instances[0].splash([\n        { x: 200, y: 200, radius: 250, velocity: 1.4 },\n        { x: 900, y: 500, radius: 100, velocity: 0.6 },\n      ]);\n      instances[1].splash([{ x: 576, y: 324, radius: Then sync calls \u2014 no awaiting, no postMessage, no iframe.\n- Chaining works: `inst.setBackground('sunset').setVisualization('pulse')`. Every API method that doesn't return a value (most of them) returns the instance.\n- Three global buttons (splash both, re-wet both, reset both) and per-canvas click handlers (splash just one) \u2014 same `inst.splash()` API used both ways.\n\nThe container needs `position: relative` and `overflow: hidden` \u2014 the library mounts its canvas absolute-positioned at `inset: 0`. That's the only structural requirement on the host page.\n\nThree patterns the example demonstrates that you'll probably want in real code:\n\n1. **Configure on init**: `inst.setBackground(...)` immediately after `create()`. Synchronous.\n2. **Per-instance interactivity**: store the handle from `create()` in a variable, attach DOM event listeners that call methods on it.\n3. **Re-apply config after reset**: `reset()` wipes sim state including animations and backgrounds, so chain them back on if you want them.",

The parent can disable pointer painting per-instance via\n// options.pointer === false.\n\nconst _pointerEnabled = options.pointer !== false;\nlet _isPainting = false;\nlet _lastGX = 0, _lastGY = 0;\nlet _pendingPaintX = null, _pendingPaintY = null;\n\nfunction _clientToGrid(e) {\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const cx = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;\n  const cy = (e.clientY - rect.top)  / rect.height;\n  return { gx: cx * GW, gy: cy * GH };\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerDown(e) {\n  if (!_pointerEnabled) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  _isPainting = true;\n  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  // brushSize is in display pixels \u2014 convert to grid radius. Halve for\n  // radius (vs diameter), divide by SCALE for grid units.\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  canvas.setPointerCapture && canvas.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerMove(e) {\n  if (!_isPainting || !_pointerEnabled) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  // Queue the latest position; the main loop's applyPendingPaint\n  // interpolates from lastGX/lastGY to here, stamping along the path.\n  const { gx, gy } = _clientToGrid(e);\n  _pendingPaintX = gx;\n  _pendingPaintY = gy;\n}\n\nfunction _onPointerUp(e) {\n  if (!_isPainting) return;\n  _isPainting = false;\n  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  _pendingPaintY = null;\n  canvas.releasePointerCapture && canvas.releasePointerCapture(e.pointerId);\n}\n\n// Interpolated paint: along the path from _lastG to _pendingPaint,\n// stamp every ~brushSize/2 pixels worth of distance. Without this, fast\n// drags would skip cells and produce broken strokes.\nfunction applyPendingPaint() {\n  if (_pendingPaintX === null) return;\n  const gx = _pendingPaintX, gy = _pendingPaintY;\n  const r = (brushSize / 2) / SCALE;\n  const stampSpacing = Math.max(0.5, r * 0.4);   // grid units between stamps\n  const dx = gx - _lastGX, dy = gy - _lastGY;\n  const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\n  if (dist < stampSpacing) {\n    paintAt(gx, gy, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n  } else {\n    const steps = Math.ceil(dist / stampSpacing);\n    for (let s = 1; s <= steps; s++) {\n      const f = s / steps;\n      paintAt(_lastGX + dx * f, _lastGY + dy * f, r, currentPigment, 0.7);\n    }\n  }\n  _lastGX = gx; _lastGY = gy;\n  _pendingPaintX = null;\n  _pendingPaintY = null;\n}\n\nif (_pointerEnabled) {\n  // Pointer events unify mouse + touch + pen on modern browsers.\n  canvas.style.touchAction = 'none';   // prevent scroll on touch drag\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', _onPointerDown);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', _onPointerMove);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerup',   _onPointerUp);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointercancel', _onPointerUp);\n  canvas.addEventListener('pointerleave',  _onPointerUp);\n}\n\n\n",

<b>watercolor-lib.js</b></div>\n\n<script src=\"watercolor-lib.js\"></script>\n<script>\n\"use strict\";\n\n// ==========================================================\n// v0.27 \u2014 UI WIRING (entire app is ~150 lines below)\n// ==========================================================\n// The simulation lives in watercolor-lib.js. This script does\n// nothing more than create one instance and wire each UI\n// element to a method on that instance. Compare to v0.26.2,\n// which had ~5800 lines of inline sim code; here the UI side\n// is small and the lib side is reusable.\n\nconst host = document.getElementById('canvas-host');\n// canvasScale: 1 forces no auto-downscaling \u2014 this is a full-viewport\n// canvas, the same context the brush/animation values were tuned for.\nconst wc = Watercolor.create(host, { canvasScale: 1 });\n\n// ----- Pigment swatches -----\nconst swatches = document.querySelectorAll('.swatch');\nswatches.forEach(sw => {\n  sw.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const p = parseInt(sw.dataset.pigment, 10);\n    wc.pigment(p);\n    swatches.forEach(s => s.classList.toggle('active', s === sw));\n  });\n});\n\n// ----- Brush size, paint load, water load -----\nfunction wireSlider(id, outId, fn, fmt) {\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\n  const out = document.getElementById(outId);\n  el.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    const v = parseFloat(el.value);\n    fn(v);\n    out.textContent = fmt ? fmt(v) : v;\n  });\n}\nwireSlider('size',       'size-out',       v => wc.brushSize(v));\nwireSlider('paint-load', 'paint-load-out', v => wc.paintLoad(v), v => v.toFixed(1) + '\u00d7');\nwireSlider('water-load', 'water-load-out', v => wc.waterLoad(v), v => v.toFixed(1) + '\u00d7');\n\n// ----- Background / animation / visualization selects -----\ndocument.getElementById('background').addEventListener('change', e => {\n  if (e.target.value) wc.setBackground(e.target.value);\n});\ndocument.getElementById('animation').addEventListener('change', e => {\n  wc.setAnimation(e.target.value || 'off');\n});\ndocument.getElementById('visualization').addEventListener('change', e => {\n  wc.setVisualization(e.target.value || 'off');\n});\n\n// ----- Action buttons -----\nfunction flashBtn(btn) {\n  btn.classList.add('on');\n  setTimeout(() => btn.classList.remove('on'), 220);\n}\ndocument.getElementById('rewet').addEventListener('click', e => {\n  wc.rewet(); flashBtn(e.currentTarget);\n});\ndocument.getElementById('big-splash').addEventListener('click', e => {\n  wc.splash('bigSplash'); flashBtn(e.currentTarget);\n});\ndocument.getElementById('fine-spritz').addEventListener('click', e => {\n  wc.splash('fineSpritz'); flashBtn(e.currentTarget);\n});\ndocument.getElementById('dry').addEventListener('click', e => {\n  wc.dry(); flashBtn(e.currentTarget);\n});\ndocument.getElementById('reset').addEventListener('click', e => {\n  wc.reset(); flashBtn(e.currentTarget);\n});\ndocument.getElementById('export').addEventListener('click', e => {\n  const url = wc.exportPNG();\n  // Trigger a download via an anchor.\n  const a = document.createElement('a');\n  a.href = url;\n  a.download = 'watercolor.png';\n  a.click();\n  flashBtn(e.currentTarget);\n});\n\n// ----- Toggles -----\nfunction wireToggle(id, getter, setter) {\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\n  el.classList.toggle('on', !!getter());\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    setter(!getter());\n    el.classList.toggle('on', !!getter());\n  });\n}\nwireToggle('t-edge',    () => wc.edgeDarkening(),     v => wc.edgeDarkening(v));\nwireToggle('t-fade',    () => wc.fadePainting(),      v => wc.fadePainting(v));\nwireToggle('t-pause',   () => wc.pauseDrying(),       v => wc.pauseDrying(v));\nwireToggle('t-autodry', () => wc.autoDryBackground(), v => wc.autoDryBackground(v));\n\n// ----- Dryness indicator -----\n// Periodic state poll. The lib doesn't expose wetness directly yet,\n// so this is just a placeholder hooked to backgroundRunning as a\n// proxy for \"stuff is happening\". For v0.27 it's purely decorative.\nsetInterval(() => {\n  const s = wc.state();\n  const running = s.backgroundRunning ||\n                   s.animationMode !== 'off' ||\n                   s.visualizationMode !== 'off';\n  document.getElementById('dryness').style.width = running ? '100%' : '20%';\n}, 400);\n\n// ----- Boot: paint a couple of dabs so the canvas isn't blank -----\n// The library's per-instance init starts with an empty wet canvas.\n// Drop two seed splotches similar to the v0.26.2 initial state.\nconst w = wc.grid.width, h = wc.grid.height;\nwc.paintAt(w * 0.40, h * 0.50, w * 0.04, 0, 0.7);\nwc.paintAt(w * 0.60, h * 0.50, w * 0.04, 2, 0.7);\n</script>\n\n</body>\n</html>\n",

setter) {\\n  const el = document.getElementById(id);\\n  el.classList.toggle('on', !!getter());\\n  el.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n    setter(!getter());\\n    el.classList.toggle('on', !!getter());\\n  });\\n}\\nwireToggle('t-edge',    () => wc.edgeDarkening(),     v => wc.edgeDarkening(v));\\nwireToggle('t-fade',    () => wc.fadePainting(),      v => Save them in the same directory.**\n\n## Library v2 (`watercolor-lib.js`)\n\n**The brush-scale fix you noticed.** Library now auto-computes a `_canvasScale` factor from the display width relative to a 1200px reference. Verified in the test:\n\n| Canvas size | canvasScale | Effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| 1500px (full viewport) | 1.00 | Original tuning, no change |\n| 320px (small card) | 0.28 | Brushes ~3.6\u00d7 smaller, visually proportional |\n\nApplied to `paintAt` (all internal brush calls \u2014 animations, visualizations, time washes) and `splash` (radius). Caller can override via `Watercolor.create(el, { canvasScale: 1.5 })` or `inst.canvasScale(0.5)`.\n\n**New API surface in v2:**\n\n- **Pointer painting** \u2014 mouse + touch on the canvas, with mid-stroke interpolation. Disable per-instance with `{ pointer: false }`.\n- **Brush controls** \u2014 `inst.brushSize(px)`, `inst.pigment(0|1|2|'water'|'lift'|'rainbow'|'mask')`, `inst.pigments()` (list available)\n- **Direct paint** \u2014 `inst.paintAt(gx, gy, gridRadius, pigmentIdx, strength)` for choreographed scripts\n- **More toggles** \u2014 `pauseDrying`, `autoDryBackground`, `fadePainting`, `transparent`, `paintLoad(v)`, `waterLoad(v)`, `canvasScale(v)`\n- **PNG export** \u2014 `inst.exportPNG()` returns a data URL\n- **Expanded `state()`** \u2014 includes brushSize, pigment, canvasScale, and toggle states\n\nAll v1 APIs still work \u2014 additive, no breaking changes.\n\n## v0.27 \u2014 Standalone driven by the lib\n\nThe whole tool is now ~150 lines of UI wiring at the bottom of the HTML, plus the layout markup and styles. Compare to v0.26.2 which had ~5,800 lines of inline simulation code. The sim moved to the library; this file is the **UI**.\n\nWhat's wired:\n- Pigment swatches (3 pigments + water + lift + rainbow)\n- Brush size, paint load, water load sliders\n- Background / animation / visualization dropdowns\n- Re-wet / Big splash / Fine spritz / Dry / Reset / Export PNG buttons\n- Edge darkening / Fade painting / Pause drying / Auto-dry bg toggles\n- Pointer painting on the canvas\n\nWhat's deferred (these were in v0.26.2 but not yet in the lib):\n- WebGL render path\n- Perf overlay\n- Text painting + SVG tracing\n- Paper wetness presets\n- Mask brush + Remove mask\n- Theme / paper color picker\n- Documentation section\n- Keyboard shortcuts\n- Cursor preview element (the floating brush indicator)\n\nv0.26.2 still exists as the full-featured standalone. v0.27 is the \"library-eats-its-own-dogfood\" version \u2014 proof the factory pattern works end-to-end. Adding deferred features now happens in one place (the library) rather than two.\n\n`canvasScale: 1` is passed explicitly to `Watercolor.create()` in v0.27 because the canvas fills the viewport and matches the original tuning \u2014 auto-scaling would shrink brushes unnecessarily.",

That guarantees b\u00e9ziers, arcs, and other curves\n  6002\t// trace at consistent visual spacing regardless of their parametric\n  6003\t// speed \u2014 uniform brushstroke density along every curve.\n  6004\t//\n  6005\t// All sampled points are queued. Each frame paints SVG_POINTS_PER_FRAME\n  6006\t// of them through paintAt, using whatever brush the user has active\n  6007\t// (currentPigment). The user watches the SVG draw itself with the\n  6008\t// watercolor brush, getting the bleed/edge/granulation features of the\n  6009\t// sim along every stroke.\n  6010\t//\n  6011\t// Scale: SVG fits into ~70% of canvas, preserving aspect ratio, centered.\n  6012\t//\n  6013\t// Limitations: parent-group transforms (e.g. <g transform=\"rotate()\">)\n  6014\t// are not applied \u2014 paths are read from their raw `d` attributes. SVGs\n  6015\t// authored without nested transforms (typical exports from vector tools)\n  6016\t// trace correctly. Strokes/fills from the SVG itself aren't used; the\n  6017\t// active brush determines the color/behavior.\n  6018\t\n  6019\tconst SVG_TRACE_FIT = 0.70;           // fraction of canvas the SVG fills\n  6020\tconst SVG_POINTS_PER_FRAME = 10;      // trace speed\n  6021\tconst SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS = 2.0; // sample every ~2 grid cells along each path\n  6022\tconst SVG_TRACE_STRENGTH = 0.42;\n  6023\tconst SVG_TRACE_BRUSH_PX = 9;\n  6024\t\n  6025\tlet svgTraceState = null;             // { points: [{x,y}], idx, pigment, brushPx, strength }\n  6026\t\n  6027\t// Convert any single SVG geometry element to an equivalent path \"d\"\n  6028\t// string.       "json_block": "{\"language\": \"html\", \"code\": \"// ============================================================\\n// SVG TRACING (v0.17)\\n// ============================================================\\n// User picks an SVG file; the parser walks every geometry element\\n// (path / line / polyline / polygon / rect / circle / ellipse),\\n// converts each to a path-d string, and uses the browser's built-in\\n// SVGPathElement.getPointAtLength to sample arc-length-parametrized\\n// points along it. That guarantees b\\u00e9ziers, arcs, and other curves\\n// trace at consistent visual spacing regardless of their parametric\\n// speed \\u2014 uniform brushstroke density along every curve.\\n//\\n// All sampled points are queued. Each frame paints SVG_POINTS_PER_FRAME\\n// of them through paintAt, using whatever brush the user has active\\n// (currentPigment). The user watches the SVG draw itself with the\\n// watercolor brush, getting the bleed/edge/granulation features of the\\n// sim along every stroke.\\n//\\n// Scale: SVG fits into ~70% of canvas, preserving aspect ratio, centered.\\n//\\n// Limitations: parent-group transforms (e.g. <g transform=\\\"rotate()\\\">)\\n// are not applied \\u2014 paths are read from their raw `d` attributes. SVGs\\n// authored without nested transforms (typical exports from vector tools)\\n// trace correctly. Strokes/fills from the SVG itself aren't used; the\\n// active brush determines the color/behavior.\\n\\nconst SVG_TRACE_FIT = 0.70;           // fraction of canvas the SVG fills\\nconst SVG_POINTS_PER_FRAME = 10;      // trace speed\\nconst SVG_SAMPLE_DENSITY_CELLS = 2.0; // sample every ~2 grid cells along each path\\nconst SVG_TRACE_STRENGTH = 0.42;\\nconst SVG_TRACE_BRUSH_PX = 9;\\n\\nlet svgTraceState = null;             // { points: [{x,y}], idx, pigment, brushPx, strength }\\n\\n// Convert any single SVG geometry element to an equivalent path \\\"d\\\"\\n// string. Lets us treat lines/polygons/rects/circles/ellipses uniformly\\n// through SVGPathElement.getPointAtLength.\\nfunction pathStringFromSvgElement(el) {\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"

Each maps to a multiplier that scales the\\n// underlying evaporation rate via rate = 0.9988^mult.\\n//\\n// \\\"Wet-on-wet\\\" is the default \u2014 it corresponds to mult=3, slightly\\n// faster than the original Curtis 0.9988 baseline (mult=1, \\\"Flooded\\\").\\n// The shift is intentional: the original baseline keeps the canvas\\n// wet for ~9.5 seconds, which feels indefinite in interactive use.
